*Information about this image and its acquisition is forthcoming.*

The Moon, Earth's only natural satellite, orbits at an average distance of about 384,400 km (238,900 miles), spans roughly 31 arcminutes across the sky near full phase, and can reach an apparent magnitude of about -12.7 at full moon — bright enough to cast shadows at night.

*This description was generated by an AI assistant from general astronomical references, not from Jay's own notes on this image — treat it as a starting point, not a verified caption.*
